body:
## Summary

Adds `Base.show`/`Base.summary` methods across ClimaLand's core types so that inspecting a model, domain, driver, or parameter set interactively in the REPL produces a short, readable summary instead of Julia's default field dump — which, for these deeply-parameterized types, could run into the *millions* of characters (`PrescribedAtmosphere` alone was 22.8M characters by default).

The types covered fall into a few families:

- **Domains** (`Point`, `Column`, `Plane`, `HybridBox`, `SphericalShell`, `SphericalSurface`): bespoke per-type formatting of bounds, resolution, optional mesh stretching, and compute device.
- **Drivers** (`PrescribedAtmosphere`, `PrescribedRadiativeFluxes`): show start date, height/gustiness, and what *kind* of driver populates each field (e.g. analytic function vs. ERA5-interpolated data) instead of dumping the underlying NetCDF-backed data.
- **Models** (generic `AbstractModel` fallback + `LandModel` override, generic `AbstractCanopyComponent` fallback): show the model's domain and prognostic variable names instead of just a bare type name.
- **Boundary conditions** (generic `ClimaLand.AbstractBC` fallback, covering every BC type package-wide) and **runoff models** (`AbstractRunoffModel`): describe prescribed functions rather than dumping opaque closures, and avoid leaking raw `ClimaCore.Field` data.
- **Parameter structs** (`LandParameters`, `EnergyHydrologyParameters`, `RichardsParameters`, `TwoStreamParameters`, `SnowParameters`, `BucketModelParameters`, `PrescribedBaregroundAlbedo`/`PrescribedSurfaceAlbedo`): show headline parameters and nested closure/albedo "kind" instead of every scalar field plus fully-expanded nested structs.

## Implementation note

All of the `Base.show`/`Base.summary` methods above (and the small presentation-only helper functions they share, e.g. `_scalar_or_field_str`, `_kind_str`, `_driver_kind`) live in a single `src/shared_utilities/show_methods.jl`, included last in `ClimaLand.jl` after every submodule is loaded, rather than being scattered beside each type definition. Types owned by submodules that aren't `using`'d into the top-level `ClimaLand` namespace (`Bucket`, `Canopy`, `Soil.Runoff`) are referenced there via their qualified path (e.g. `Bucket.BucketModelParameters`); this is confirmed not to trip `Aqua.test_piracies` since those submodules are still part of the `ClimaLand` package.

## Before / after examples

**`PrescribedAtmosphere`** (ERA5-forced, real global run) — default `repr` was **22.8 million characters**:
```
PrescribedAtmosphere{Float64}
  start_date: 2005-01-01T00:00:00
  reference height: 1.0 m, gustiness: 1.0 m/s
  drivers: InterpolatingTimeVaryingInput23D (liquid/snow precip, T, u, q, P)
  CO2: AnalyticTimeVaryingInput
```

**`SphericalShell`** domain:
```
SphericalShell{Float64}
  radius: 6.3781e6 m, depth: 50.0 m
  nelements: (101, 15) (horizontal, vertical), npolynomial: 0
  mesh stretching: (dz_bottom=10.0, dz_top=0.05) m
  device: CPUSingleThreaded
```

**`LandModel`** — previously fell back to Julia's default struct dump (would recursively print every sub-model, each of which embeds the same noisy atmos/radiation type parameters):
```
LandModel{Float64}
  domain: SphericalShell{Float64}(radius=6.3781e6 m, depth=50.0 m, 101×15 elements)
├── soilco2: SoilCO2Model
├── soil: EnergyHydrology
├── canopy: CanopyModel
└── snow: SnowModel
```

**`soil.parameters`** (`EnergyHydrologyParameters`) — default `repr` was **18,350 characters**:
```
EnergyHydrologyParameters{Float64}
  porosity (ν): Field, K_sat: Field
  S_s: Field, θ_r: Field
  hydrology closure: vanGenuchten
  albedo: CLMTwoBandSoilAlbedo
  earth_param_set: LandParameters{Float64}
```

**`soil.boundary_conditions.top`** (`AtmosDrivenFluxBC`) — default `repr` was **5,467 characters** (the atmos/radiation *values* would already print compactly, but the struct's own type-parameter header spelled them out in full regardless):
```
AtmosDrivenFluxBC
  atmos: PrescribedAtmosphere{Float64}(start_date=2005-01-01T00:00:00)
  radiation: PrescribedRadiativeFluxes{Float64}(start_date=2005-01-01T00:00:00)
  runoff: TOPMODELRunoff
  prognostic_land_components: (:canopy, :snow, :soil, :soilco2)
```

**`canopy.radiative_transfer`** (`TwoStreamModel`) — default `repr` was **7,924 characters**:
```
TwoStreamModel{Float64}
  parameters: TwoStreamParameters{Float64}
```

## Test plan
- [x] Added `Base.show`/`Base.summary` unit tests in `test/shared_utilities/domains.jl`, `test/shared_utilities/drivers.jl`, and `test/integrated/full_land.jl`
- [x] `test/shared_utilities/domains.jl` passes (21/21 new assertions, no regressions)
- [x] `test/shared_utilities/drivers.jl` passes (14/14 new assertions, no regressions)
- [x] `test/integrated/full_land.jl` passes (28/28 new assertions across FT=Float32/Float64, no regressions)
- [x] `Aqua.detect_ambiguities` passes — no method ambiguities introduced by the new generic fallbacks
- [x] `Aqua.test_piracies` passes after consolidating all show methods (including the cross-submodule ones) into `show_methods.jl`
- [x] `test/standalone/Soil/runoff.jl`, `soil_bc.jl`, `parameters.jl` pass
- [x] `test/standalone/Vegetation/test_two_stream.jl`, `test_pmodel.jl` pass
- [x] `test/standalone/Snow/parameters.jl` passes
- [x] `test/standalone/Bucket/albedo_types.jl` passes
- [x] Verified interactively in a Julia REPL against real constructions (both ERA5-forced and analytic-forcing) for all affected types
- [x] `JuliaFormatter` run on all changed files

title: Add Base.show/summary methods for noisy REPL types
